Name: Agatha
Bio: Agatha is a retired caregiver in her sixties, living in the state. She spent 1 - 2 years caring for a loved one. As a parent, balancing her own needs with the responsibilities of providing constant support is vital. Financial concerns and emotional strain have been a significant part of her experience, but her deep commitment to ensuring her loved one’s well-being kept her going.
Behavior 1: Spends an average of 5 hours per week on caregiving duties.
Behavior 2: Relies on family contributions and government aid where available.
Behavior 3: Feels emotionally and physically exhausted but continues out of duty and love.
Behavior 4: Looks for guidance on handling caregiver burnout and emotional stress.
Behavior 5: Participates in local caregiver support groups or online communities when possible.
Core Needs 1: Needs financial and emotional support to ease the burden of caregiving.
Core Needs 2: Wants access to respite care and community programs for additional assistance.
Core Needs 3: Seeks reliable information on financial aid and healthcare services.
Core Needs 4: Values the ability to balance caregiving with personal well-being and self-care.
Core Needs 5: Worries about not doing enough and seeks reassurance in her caregiving efforts.
